questions.question: Explain the difference between mkdir and mkdir -p with examples.
keyPoints: mkdir creates directory only if parent exists, fails otherwise
mkdir -p doesn't error if directory already exists
Example: mkdir a/b fails if 'a' doesn't exist but mkdir -p a/b succeeds

questions.question: Write the mkdir command to create a directory named 'projects' with permissions 755.
keyPoints: 755 gives owner full access, others read+execute
Command creates directory in current location

questions.question: What is the purpose of the -v option in mkdir command? Give an example.
keyPoints: Prints message for each directory created
Example: mkdir -v mydir outputs 'mkdir: created directory 'mydir''

questions.question: How do you create multiple directories in a single command? Give an example.
keyPoints: List directory names separated by spaces
Creates all three directories in current location
Useful for setting up project structures quickly

questions.question: What happens when you try to create an already existing directory without using any option?
keyPoints: Error: 'mkdir: cannot create directory: File exists'
Use -p flag to avoid this error

questions.question: Explain permission mode 755 and what access it provides to owner, group, and others.
keyPoints: This is the standard default permission for directories

questions.question: Explain the mkdir command in Linux with its syntax, various options, and practical examples.
Write and explain commands to: (a) Create nested directories /home/user/projects/app/src (b) Create directory with only owner access (c) Create multiple directories with verbose output.
keyPoints: Explain each option used and expected output
Show how -p avoids errors for existing paths
Demonstrate permission settings and verbose mode

questions.question: What are directory permissions in Linux? Explain with mkdir -m option and give examples for different permission levels.
keyPoints: Directories need execute permission to access contents
Explain rwx representation and numeric values
Practical scenarios for each permission type

questions.question: Compare mkdir with and without -p option. Also explain how to create directories with specific permissions using -m option with examples.
keyPoints: Without -p: fails if parent doesn't exist
With -p: no error if directory exists
